assumption_checks.impact: This really is an execute-and-review phase: the checkout already contains the dedicated comment-post success test, the shared five-route header breadth loop, and the split disabled-limiter resolve sentinel in `tests/test_bayes_market_api.py`.

The meaningful audit baseline is `ff/Tbayes-market-u44-execute-step-2`; comparing to `ff/Tbayes-market-u44-execute-step-3` hides the implementation diff and would produce a misleadingly empty coverage review.

The scope proof should stay tests-only among product files. The shared runtime header path already exists in `backend/server.py`, so step 5 should confirm that no backend runtime file moved relative to the step-2 baseline.

The selector needs one more test than the assumption named. To close out step 4 as well as step 3, the focused run should include both resolve split tests: `test_market_resolve_http_requires_valid_agent_id_when_enabled` and `test_market_resolve_http_omits_rate_limit_headers_when_limiter_disabled`.
No child task is needed. The changed tests, the existing shared header runtime path, and the branch topology are all visible locally, and the focused execution slice already passed in this checkout.
